formula_note: Baseline civilian direct-death numerator set to the officially confirmed 'at least 45' civilian deaths reported at battle end.
uncertainty_note: This is almost certainly a floor because missing bodies and rubble deaths were repeatedly flagged by reporters and residents, but stronger city-bounded direct-death totals were not located.
quality_note: Usable for a conservative baseline only.

formula_note: Aggregate combatant deaths set to 920 militants plus 165 troops and police killed.
uncertainty_note: Official total is clean and battle-bounded, but it merges all state-force components and may still omit post-battle recoveries.
quality_note: Strong enough for the baseline DR numerator.

formula_note: No direct unknown-status death total was supportable. The 80 male residents possibly forced to fight are kept as ambiguous status context and are not silently moved into military or civilian tallies.
uncertainty_note: Unknown-status persons existed in the battle pocket, but no death total for them was located.
quality_note: Unknown-status deaths remain unresolved and excluded from the baseline DR.

uncertainty_note: Largest uncertainty is how quickly the whole-city remainder collapsed into the much smaller trapped/hostage pocket after the first week.
quality_note: Battle-bounded and explicit, but the mid-June transition from whole-city remainder to trapped-core population is assumption-heavy.

uncertainty_note: Late troop exposure depends partly on a search-result snippet and early 23 May simultaneous state-force deployment is not directly observed.
quality_note: Reasonable city-theater force exposure estimate, but not a precise muster roll.

formula_note: Computed as (mil_deaths_direct / mil_person_months) / (civ_deaths_direct / civ_person_months), using direct violent deaths only and strict non-allocation of ambiguous-status persons. [corrected: formula was previously inverted (civ_rate/mil_rate); now uses standard mil_rate/civ_rate per definitions.md]
uncertainty_note: Best read as a conservative baseline DR because the civilian death numerator is likely a floor while the denominator is relatively well captured.
quality_note: Publishable as a cautious baseline, not as an exhaustive measure of civilian harm.
